priority_actions.action: Report existing SGD baseline results — analyze SGD data already collected, generate comparison tables (SGD vs AdamW), run paired t-tests, show that WD matters under SGD (constant 91.22% vs no_wd 90.30%, Delta=0.92%) directly validating optimizer-specificity claim

details.key_finding: SGD shows ~0.92% gap between constant and no_wd on CIFAR-10 vs ~0.12% under AdamW — 8x larger effect
details.paper_impact: Transforms paper from pure null result to sharp optimizer-dependent characterization

priority_actions.action: Add ImageNet experiments with ResNet-50 — at least 4 key methods (constant, cosine_schedule, cwd_hard, no_wd) × 3 seeds, 90 epochs

details.rationale: Required by project spec; validates scale-dependence claim; most reviewers flag this as critical gap

priority_actions.action: Fix all three metric definitions: (1) BEM — fix boundedness claim, investigate half_lambda BEM=0.000 bug, use signed BEM; (2) AIS — correct range to [-1,1], specify Delta-L sign convention; (3) CSI — add component normalization, justify or ablate weights

bem_fixes: Remove [0,1] bound claim or restrict domain
Consider signed BEM to preserve directional info
csi_fixes: Justify weights via sensitivity analysis or use equal (1/3, 1/3, 1/3)

priority_actions.action: Add VGG-16-BN on CIFAR-10/100 as second architecture — all 7 methods × 3 seeds × 2 datasets = 42 runs

details.methods: all 7 (constant, cosine_schedule, cwd_hard, cwd_soft, swd, half_lambda, no_wd)
details.rationale: Tests architecture dependence of Phi Invariance — single architecture is the most cited weakness

priority_actions.action: Increase seeds to 5 for all configurations — add seeds 789 and 999

details.statistical_benefit: Reduces minimum detectable effect from ~0.7% to ~0.45%

priority_actions.action: Prove Phi Invariance under quadratic loss with diagonal Hessian under AdamW — formal theorem providing theoretical support for conjecture
Add quantitative order-of-magnitude analysis of Phi perturbation magnitude vs adaptive gradient step magnitude across training
Add formal power analysis stating minimum detectable effect size; add TOST equivalence testing with ±0.3% margin using 5 seeds; add bootstrap 95% CI
Address BN scale-invariance confound — either test a BN-free architecture (VGG-16 without BN or MLP) or discuss explicitly as a confound with mechanistic reasoning
Add training curves (loss and accuracy vs epoch, mean ± std) and weight norm trajectory plots for key methods
Add Discussion subsection engaging with implicit regularization literature — cite Wilson et al. 2017, Barrett & Dherin 2021, Kosson et al. 2023, Smith et al. 2021
Test higher lambda values (5e-3, 5e-2) for constant, cosine_schedule, cwd_hard on CIFAR-10 to distinguish 'AdamW subsumes WD' from 'WD too small to matter'

details.rationale: Codex review's key insight — at lambda=5e-4, WD is ~1% perturbation; need to test when WD is first-order

priority_actions.action: Unify terminology throughout paper — use framework axis names (temporal, directional, spatial, target-norm) consistently from Introduction
Expand conclusion to 300+ words covering stability finding, TOST caveats, benchmark infrastructure contribution, SGD boundary condition
Create referenced appendices (Appendix B: diagnostic panels for all runs, Appendix C.2: CSI sensitivity analysis)
Add reproducibility table with hardware specs, software versions, wall-clock times, codebase URL
Strengthen conjecture statement — cover full BEM range, define 'sufficiently overparameterized' quantitatively, make boundary conditions formal
Add computational overhead comparison for dynamic vs constant WD methods
Investigate and discuss cosine_schedule variance anomaly (sigma=0.07% on CIFAR-10 but 0.42% on CIFAR-100)

new_experiments: Additional seeds (789, 999): 7 methods × 2 seeds × 2 datasets for ResNet-20 (P0-5)
fix_existing: Analyze and report existing SGD baseline data (P0-1)
Generate training curves from existing run logs (P1-5)
Extract weight norm trajectories from existing checkpoints (P1-5)
experiment_plan.parallelization_strategy: ImageNet + VGG-16-BN + additional seeds can run concurrently on 8 GPUs

theory_plan: Prove Phi Invariance Theorem for quadratic loss f(w) = 0.5 w^T H w under AdamW with diagonal H (P1-1)
Derive quantitative bound: ||phi(t,w,g) * lambda * w|| / ||m_t / (sqrt(v_t) + eps)|| = O(lambda / lr) << 1 (P1-2)
Connect to implicit regularization: show AdamW's effective regularization path subsumes explicit WD modulation (P1-6)
Formalize the 'BN scale-invariance' confound and its interaction with WD irrelevance (P1-4)
writing_plan: Section 4: Add ImageNet and VGG-16-BN experiment descriptions
Section 5.1: Update all accuracy tables with 5-seed results, add SGD comparison table
Section 5.2: Fix BEM definition and recompute all BEM values
Section 5.3: Fix AIS/CSI definitions, add CIFAR-100 diagnostic table
Section 5 (new): Add training curves and weight norm trajectories subsection
Section 3: Add Theorem 1 (quadratic loss invariance proof)
Section 3.4: Fix all metric mathematical definitions
Section 6: Rewrite Discussion with implicit regularization literature engagement
Appendix: Create B (diagnostic panels), C.2 (CSI sensitivity), per-seed tables
Throughout: Unify terminology to framework axis names
lessons: Always report ALL collected experimental data — the SGD baseline was the single most impactful unreported result
Metric definitions require formal verification of boundary cases before publication — all 3 metrics had mathematical errors
Null result papers require STRONGER experimental coverage than positive result papers — reviewers will ask 'did you just not look hard enough?'
3 seeds is never sufficient for null-result claims — budget for 5+ seeds from the planning phase
A notational framework without deeper theoretical results is seen as 'just notation' — need at least one non-trivial theorem
Address confounds proactively (BN scale-invariance, small-lambda regime) rather than waiting for reviewers to identify them
Plan parallel experiment execution from the start — SGD and VGG experiments could have run during Iteration 3 writing
Codex review provides a genuinely orthogonal perspective — the 'WD too small to matter' insight was unique to Codex

risk_factors.risk: ImageNet experiments fail or show unexpected results
risk_factors.mitigation: If dynamic WD helps at ImageNet scale, pivot framing to 'scale-dependent transition' rather than universal invariance

risk_factors.risk: VGG-16-BN shows different pattern from ResNet-20
risk_factors.mitigation: Reframe as architecture-dependent finding; strengthen Discussion with architecture analysis
Prioritize P0-1 (SGD analysis, zero compute) and P0-3 (metric fixes) first; experiments can extend to Iteration 5

risk_factors.risk: Higher lambda experiments break the invariance
risk_factors.mitigation: This is actually a positive finding — establishes boundary conditions for the conjecture, making it more precise and informative
